name: From Agent Failures to Text Policies: What Works and What Breaks

description: arXiv:2607.20668v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: TextGrad improves language-model systems by revising text from feedback. Its core thesis is that natural-language feedback can act as a gradient for optimizing text components without changing model weights. Applying it to agents is harder because feedback arrives only after a sequence of actions, making it difficult to identify which decision c...
author: Jaideep Ray, Ankit Goyal
